steps.title: Compare the two views
steps.content: Text 1 says Luttrell's reputation should be considered questionable because of his actions against Sempringham Priory. Text 2 does not deny the conflict but explains it as part of a property dispute in which Luttrell believed he had a right to the property.

steps.title: Reject unsupported responses
steps.content:
• Choice A says the Psalter outweighs the misconduct, but Text 2 does not make that cultural-value argument.
  • Choice B says Luttrell was wrong despite legal permission, which Text 2 does not claim.
  • Choice C connects the actions to documentation in the Psalter, which Text 2 never mentions.
The remaining choice adds the context that Luttrell thought his actions were justified. The answer is D.
